visual_attributes.morphology: Large wild goat with a robust body and long legs
distinctive_features: Sexual dimorphism with males having longer hair on chin, throat, chest, and shanks
Females are redder with shorter hair and a short black beard
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth and short in summer, longer and thicker in winter
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 65 to 115 cm at the shoulder, 132 to 186 cm long, weighing 32 to 110 kg
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Horns close together at the head, spreading upwards toward the tips

domain_specific_visual.HORN_LENGTH: Up to 160 cm in males, up to 25 cm in females
domain_specific_visual.SEXUAL_DIMORPHISM: Males have longer hair and larger horns

secondary_functions: Symbol of national pride in Pakistan

domain_specific_functional.DIETARY_SHIFT: Grazes in spring and summer, browses in winter
domain_specific_functional.MATING_BEHAVIOR: Males fight by lunging and locking horns during mating season

relationships.description: Snow leopards are one of the main predators of the markhor.
Himalayan wolves are predators of the markhor.
Some breeds of domestic goats are believed to have descended from the markhor.

contextual_attributes.origin: Mountain regions of Central and South Asia
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Afghanistan, Pakistan, India
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: National animal of Pakistan, symbolizing strength and resilience
